data.title: Treasury Yields Fall Again Amid Calmer TradeMarket Talk
data.description:
1546 ET - Treasury yields fall for the second consecutive day as Wall Street keeps its cool following last week's wild, tariff-driven moves. March import prices declined in the U.S., but the bulk of it was due to falling energy cots. Manufacturing declined less than expected in the New York state, according to the Fed's April survey. Tomorrow, March retail sales are forecast to increase 1.2%, accelerating from February's 0.2%, in a WSJ consensus forecast. Industrial production is expected to contract 0.1%. The 10-year yield declines 0.038 percentage point to 4.322% and the two-year falls 0.003 p.p. to 3.827%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1257 ET - Inflation in Canada may have eased in March but a broadening of inflationary pressures likely will push for a Bank of Canada pause Wednesday, Alberta Central's Charles St-Arnaud argues. CPI was much weaker than expected, and the economist says that is explained by three factors that are both temporary and volatile: the end of a federal tax holiday, lower gasoline prices, lower travel services costs. He notes that behind the 2.3% rise in CPI on-year, the share of components increasing by more than 3% rose to 41% from 35%, the highest in more than a year and well above historical norms. St-Arnaud adds the Bank of Canada's preferred core measures of inflation eased only slightly with CPI trim and CPI median averaging 2.85% growth from 2.9% a month earlier, though below 3% for a ninth consecutive month. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1206 ET - Mullen Group's acquisition of Cole Group is a good way to branch out into less tariff-stricken areas, TD Cowen's Tim James says. In a report, the analyst rates the acquisition as "slightly positive based on the exposure it provides to supply chain and international commercial trade services." James figures that with the evolving tariff and counter-tariff policies in Canada and the U.S., the acquisition of an asset-light logistics company specializing in customs brokerage and trade consulting is likely a prudent move to take advantage of the changes in trade policies. While it is still unclear how much exposure Cole has to cross-border trade, James thinks that advisory/consulting services will be helpful in these times. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1121 ET - Argentina's lifting of capital controls will likely help attract international investors to the country, as they will feel more confident they can take their money out when they need to, according to UBS. Argentina's government last week announced the end of most restrictions to the flow of foreign currencies in and out of the country, while introducing a more flexible exchange rate system that let the Argentine peso fluctuate within a certain range. A new, $20 billion credit facility by the International Monetary Fund will support the initiative. "The new [currency] regime should allow the peso to adjust and regain competitiveness, with continued disinflation efforts falling more squarely on the shoulders of more active monetary policy," the investment bank says. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1107 ET - The Bank of England is expected to cut interest rates three more times by the end of 2025, taking its key policy rate to 3.75%, Oxford Economics' Andrew Goodwin says in a note. "With the outlook for demand softening in response to U.S. tariff announcements, the case for loosening monetary policy more quickly than we previously anticipated is strong," he says. The BOE could be cautious in its approach to rate cuts, however, due to concerns about inflationary pressures, Goodwin says. Markets price in a total of 80 basis points of BOE interest rate cuts by the end of this year, LSEG data show.(miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1034 ET - Canada's lower-than-expected CPI reading for March increases the chances the Bank of Canada will cut rates by a quarter point Wednesday. But with rates now firmly within neutral territory and plenty of uncertainty about trade and fiscal policy, it is more likely officials pause to try and balance the upside risks to inflation from tariffs against the downside risks to the economy, Oxford Economics' Tony Stillo and Michael Davenport reckon. The economists expect headline inflation will drop to around 2% on-year in April thanks to the removal of the consumer carbon tax and the recent fall in global oil prices, before the U.S.-Canada trade war drives inflation to nearly 3% by the end of 2025. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1019 ET - The Bank of Canada can afford to choose an "insurance" rate cut like it did in March, argues Royal Bank of Canada's Abbey Xu. The economist points to the easing in March headline inflation to 2.3% annually from 2.6% in February as cooling gasoline and travel-tour prices more than offset a bounce back in food prices after the end of the federal tax holiday. Xu says that while inflation is hotter than the 1.9% reading ahead of the two-month tax break, the latest data suggests inflation risks ahead of tariffs were subsiding. Royal Bank still expects tariffs will hurt Canadian exporters and prices are set to rise, and it anticipates one more quarter-point cut in interest rates after Wednesday to take the overnight rate to a terminal 2.25% in June. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1016 ET - Oxford Economics cuts its U.K. growth forecast for 2026 to 0.9%, from its previous forecast of 1.5%, due to the impact of U.S. trade tariffs, it says in a note.The global economic advisory firm expects U.K. GDP to grow by 1% in 2025. "We now expect softer quarterly growth for the remainder of this year and 2026, although the U.K. should manage to avoid a recession," Oxford Economics' Andrew Goodwin says in a note. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1013 ET - At the margins the odds of another Canadian rate cut Wednesday have risen with the downward surprise in annual inflation in March and first target-consistent gains in trim and median CPI measures in eight months, Capital Economics' Thomas Ryan says. Yet with the three-month annualized pace of those core inflation measures holding uncomfortably high at 2.7% and downside risk to the economy easing as trade tensions with the U.S. de-escalate, Ryan still expects the Bank of Canada will sit tight to assess the fallout of retaliatory tariffs. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0953 ET - Canadian inflation, after a couple months of high-side surprises, caught a March break and was held down by much milder travel costs than normal, BMO Economics' Douglas Porter says "This speaks to the fact that the inflation impact of the trade war is more of a two-way street for Canada than the U.S., since Canada's tariffs are so much lighter so far, while the domestic economy is under more pressure." Porter the latest easing and the prospect of another big drop in gasoline prices in April would be a green light for the Bank of Canada to cut its policy rate Wednesday, except for the small detail that its major core measures are holding close to 3%. Add to that, policymakers are operating in the dense fog of an ever-shifting trade war, the economist adds. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0946 ET - European investment-grade and high-yield corporate bonds have attractive credit spreads, making them more favorable than their U.S. equivalents, BlackRock Investment Institute strategists say in a note. Broadly, BlackRock Investment Institute is overweight on short-dated investment-grade credit as these bonds "better compensate for interest rate risk." It is underweight on long-dated credit due to tight spreads, though prefers Europe over the U.S., the strategists say. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0946 ET - The latest Canadian inflation data suggest price are grounded for now, but probably not for long, the Canadian Chamber of Commerce's Andrew DiCapua says. The economist says the impact of new tariffs pushing prices higher should start to be seen in the coming months. At the same time, changes to carbon pricing in April will offset some of that and add to the mixed signals the Bank of Canada is having to navigate, he says. DiCapua says that if trade tensions continue to escalate, Canada is looking at the risk of a stagflationary environment with slower growth and rising prices. "With so much uncertainty around global growth, especially with tariffs introduced by the U.S., we expect the Bank to hold rates steady at tomorrow's meeting." (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: NetSpark Expands Platform With Acquisition of Firefly Telecom, Broadening Its Channel and Carrier-Agnostic Capabilities
data.description:
DALLAS, TEXAS / ACCESS Newswire / April 15, 2025 / NetSpark IP & Telecom ("NetSpark"), a telecom management and advisory technology firm that serves mid-market, enterprise, and government entities, is excited to announce the acquisition of the telecom division of Firefly IT Asset Recovery ("Firefly"). This marks NetSpark's fifth investment and represents a strategic partnership in expanding the platform's capabilities, with a dedicated focus on building a national channel program that delivers flexible, carrier-agnostic telecom solutions.

Founded in 2011, Firefly provides deep expertise as a technology resource for its customers and channel partners nationwide. Joining the NetSpark team from Firefly's leadership are industry veterans Atiyeh Abdo, Steve LaMadrid, and Aaron Nowaczyk, who were the driving force behind the development of Firefly's national channel strategy. All three will transition into leadership roles in NetSpark's carrier services division.

"We are excited to welcome Firefly Telecom to the NetSpark family," said Micah Cooksey, CEO of NetSpark. "Atiyeh, Steve, and Aaron bring deep channel experience, strong industry relationships, and a shared commitment to client success. Firefly Telecom will play a key role in expanding our reach through indirect channels and offering customers a broader array of connectivity options tailored to their needs."

As a NetSpark partner, Firefly Telecom will maintain its brand with NetSpark's support and play a key role in the scale of the Company's national channel partner program while it serves as the platform's primary vehicle for carrier-diverse, client-driven telecom solutions.

"We're thrilled to be joining NetSpark with a clear mission and strong partner-focused leadership," said Atiyeh Abdo. "This partnership allows us to grow faster, serve our customers and partners better, and bring a broader range of solutions to the market."

"Firefly was built on relationships, responsiveness, and creative problem-solving," added Steve LaMadrid. "As part of NetSpark, we can now access the scale and infrastructure to deliver even more value while staying true to our roots and our partners."

"Building a true channel-led model in telecom has been a long-time goal of mine," said Aaron Nowaczyk. "We've laid the foundation at Firefly, and with the support of NetSpark, I'm excited to take our program to the next level and help partners deliver more value to their customers."

The investment in Firefly Telecom continues NetSpark's disciplined strategy of integrating high-performing telecom and managed service companies to build a market-leading national platform of telecom-driven connectivity solutions.

About NetSpark IP & Telecom

NetSpark IP & Telecom provides tailored solutions for business networks, voice services, mobility, cybersecurity, and lifecycle management. A partnership-focused company, NetSpark is dedicated to the alignment and integration of leading businesses in the telecom, connectivity, and managed services sectors.

NetSpark's growing network unlocks new possibilities for individual business owners and their teams. NetSpark's unique core services offering and national footprint provide its partner network with unsurpassed industry knowledge and behind-the-scenes support. Potential partners are welcome to learn more by reaching out to info@Netsparktelecom.com. 

Contact Information

Robert Halik

Sr. Analyst

rhalik@tellennium.com

16786879750

SOURCE: NST Tellennium

data.title: Sector Update: Energy Stocks Rise Late Afternoon
data.description:
Energy stocks were rising late Tuesday afternoon, with the NYSE Energy Sector Index up 0.1% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) adding 0.3%.

The Philadelphia Oil Service Sector index was fractionally higher, and the Dow Jones US Utilities index rose 0.5%.

The International Energy Agency reduced its projections for global oil demand growth in 2025, as rising trade tensions weigh on economic prospects. The agency now expects an oil demand increase of 730,000 barrels a day this year, down by 300,000 barrels from its previous estimate.

Front-month West Texas Intermediate crude oil was slightly lower at $61.52 a barrel, and the global benchmark Brent crude contract shed 0.1% to $64.84 a barrel. Henry Hub natural gas futures were 0.1% lower at $3.32 per 1 million BTU.

In corporate news, DTE Energy shares rose 0.1% after it said Tuesday it started construction on the first of several new solar facilities it's building in Michigan to back Ford's (F) renewable energy goals.

Peabody Energy has halted plans to seek $500 million in private debt, which Jefferies (JEF) was marketing earlier this year to private credit firms, Bloomberg reported. Peabody shares added 2.9%.

Venture Global said it has started commercial operations at its Calcasieu Pass LNG export project . Its shares were down 0.3%.

TotalEnergies said Tuesday it expects its Q1 hydrocarbon production at the high end of its guidance range of 2.5 million to 2.55 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, up nearly 4% from a year earlier. Separately, the company said it signed a 15-year deal with Energia Natural Dominicana to deliver 400,000 tons of liquefied natural gas annually. TotalEnergies shares added 0.3%.

data.title: H&R Announces Date of First Quarter 2025 Earnings Release, Conference Call and Webcast and Declares April 2025 Distribution
data.description:
TORONTO, April 15, 2025 /CNW/ - H&R Real Estate Investment Trust ("H&R" or the "REIT") today announced that it will release its financial results for the three months ended March 31, 2025 on Wednesday, May 14, 2025. Management will host a conference call to discuss the financial results for H&R REIT on Thursday, May 15, 2025 at 9.30 a.m. Eastern Time.

Conference Call

Participants can join the call by dialing 1--800--717--1738 or 1--289--514--5100. For those unable to participate in the conference call at the scheduled time, a replay will be available approximately one hour following completion of the call. To access the archived conference call by telephone, dial 1--289--819--1325 or 1--888--660--6264 and enter the passcode 05267 followed by the "#" key. The telephone replay will be available until Thursday, May 22, 2025 at midnight.

Webcast

A live audio webcast will be available through https://www.hr-reit.com/investor-relations/#investor-events. Please connect at least 15 minutes prior to the conference call to ensure adequate time for any software download that may be required to join the webcast. The webcast will be archived on H&R's website following the call date.

Monthly Distribution Declared

H&R today declared a distribution for the month of April scheduled as follows:

 
             Distribution per    Annualized  Record date     Distribution date 
             Unit 
April 2025                $0.05       $0.60  April 30, 2025       May 15, 2025

About H&R REIT

H&R is one of Canada's largest real estate investment trusts with total assets of approximately $10.6 billion as at December 31, 2024. H&R has ownership interests in a North American portfolio comprised of high-quality residential, industrial, office and retail properties comprising over 26.0 million square feet. H&R's strategy is to create a simplified, growth-oriented business focused on residential and industrial properties in order to create sustainable long-term value for unitholders. H&R plans to sell its office and retail properties as market conditions permit. H&R's target is to be a leading owner, operator and developer of residential and industrial properties, creating value through redevelopment and greenfield development in prime locations within Toronto, and high growth U.S. sunbelt and gateway cities.

data.title: 'Term Premium' Sends a Message. Inflation Isn't What Bond Investors Fear Most.
data.description:
Karishma Vanjani

An unusual situation has unfolded in the U.S. Treasury market, where investors are expecting lower inflation in the distant future, yet demanding better returns to buy and hold bonds.

The yield on U.S. government debt maturing in 10 years slipped by 0.021 percentage point to 4.36% on Tuesday, calming down after marking its biggest weekly gain since the end of the 2001 recession last week. Sales of U.S. debt are pushing down prices and raising yields; yields and bond prices are inversely related.

Last week, Wall Street mainly blamed the surge on foreign central banks selling bonds and the unwinding of a complex, highly leveraged hedge fund strategy called the basis trade. Both were difficult-to-measure variables, partly because transactions by both groups are opaque.

Now, market watchers are looking at the s0-called 10-year Treasury term premium. It measures the extra yield investors demand to stash away money for a decade, rather than just repeatedly investing into short-term securities, such as one-month Treasury bills, for 10 years. It makes sense that investors should get more yield for locking up their money for longer because there is a higher chance that interest rates, or other important variables, would change.

How high the premium goes reflects how much risk investors see. And right now, they see plenty. The latest available figure for the premium, from Thursday, has it at 0.7052 percentage point, the highest since September 2014.

So far this month, it has risen 0.33 point, the largest increase in the first eight trading days of a month since April 2022, the Dow Jones Market Data team says. The yield climbed markedly last week, coinciding with the back-and-forth on President Donald Trump's tariffs and drama in Congress as Republicans seek to raise the cap on the federal debt.

"Perhaps the likelihood of a new debt ceiling bill that blows out the deficit plus policy uncertainty are raising the term premium on bonds," Ed Yardeni, president of Yardeni Research, a well respected independent research firm, wrote on Monday.

The term premium was also blamed for a rise in yields in January as well as distress in the bond market in late 2023.

What is notable this time is that the rise in term premium has come as the market's expectations for inflation have stayed low. The 10-year break-even inflation rate, which indicates the market's expectations for inflation, closed at 2.23% on Monday, after falling to 2.18% on Friday, the lowest in more than six months. It signals that yields are rising because of factors other than long-term inflation expectations.

"The backup in yields was entirely due to the rapid increase in the term premium...and actually not one iota from inflation expectations," wrote David Rosenberg, one of the more bearish strategists on Wall Street.

Higher yields spell opportunity for investors waiting on the sidelines for better rates, but they are a menace nonetheless. They raise borrowing costs for consumers, via mortgage and credit-card rates, and for corporate borrowers. That leaves the public with less money to spend, and boosts costs for companies, with negative implications for stocks and economic growth.

Write to Karishma Vanjani at karishma.vanjani@dowjones.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Boeing Hit From All Sides in Trump's Trade War
data.description:
By Sharon Terlep

In the global trade war, Boeing is a big loser.

Chinese officials told domestic airlines not to place new orders for Boeing jets and are requiring carriers to seek approval before taking delivery of already-ordered aircraft, according to people familiar with the matter.

The tariff turmoil keeps getting worse for America's largest exporter: Boeing's vast and fragile supply chain is grappling with the end of its decadeslong duty-free status. Boeing faces retaliatory tariffs from other countries. And airlines are bracing for a drop in demand for air travel.

The developments add new pressure to the struggling jet maker, which burned through $14 billion last year and had aimed to be cash-flow positive by the end of this year. In the long run, the new tariff landscape could give a leg up to Boeing's European archrival, Airbus.

President Trump has said his tariff blitz is intended to boost U.S. manufacturing. But the trade war is hurting — not helping — one of the few major companies manufacturing high-tech products here, said Bank of America analyst Ron Epstein.

Even if China signs off on Boeing deliveries, airlines could opt for delays to avoid hefty duties. China is forecast to be Boeing's largest market over the next two decades. Boeing would take a $1.2 billion hit if China halted all deliveries this year, according to an estimate by equity research firm Bernstein. The jet maker reported $67 billion in revenue last year. Of the 130 airplanes Boeing delivered globally this year through March, 18 went to Chinese airlines.

Few purchases are bigger than a jetliner. A single 737 MAX carries a list price of more than $100 million.

Global airlines for years have clamored for more of Boeing's planes as air travel surged after the pandemic. Now, consumer sentiment is souring and some Americans have started canceling travel plans because of rising costs and fears that the economy could slide into a recession.

Ireland's Ryanair, one of Europe's biggest airlines, is set to receive 25 Boeing 737s in August but could delay taking the planes until next spring, Michael O'Leary, the airline's CEO, said this week in a Financial Times interview.

Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg has said he is concerned about the impact of tariffs on Boeing's supply chain and the jet maker's ability to export, in addition to direct costs that would result from duties on parts and materials.

The immediate fallout of China's move is blunted by the fact that demand for Boeing planes far outpaces the jet maker's pace of production. Boeing has an order backlog of some 5,500 planes; a plane ordered today won't be delivered for close to a decade.

The longer-term impact could be more serious for Boeing's business in China, a country that represents one-fifth of global airplane demand.

China's homegrown Boeing 747 competitor, the Comac C919 jet, is years away from becoming a global force and still relies on U.S. suppliers for parts. It delivered just 13 airplanes in 2024.

But trade tensions and higher costs for U.S. planes could put Airbus, already outpacing Boeing in China, even further ahead if airlines start canceling orders. Airbus has two final-assembly lines in China; Boeing doesn't build planes there but has a center where it completes mostly-finished planes.

Boeing and its biggest suppliers rely on a huge network of suppliers, some of which are small, low-margin businesses struggling with labor shortages and higher costs. The network has been crippled by years of production stops and starts, leading to the manufacturing woes that culminated in the blowout last year of a fuselage panel on an Alaska Airlines flight.

Those suppliers face new disruptions after the upending of a trade deal that since the 1980s has allowed Boeing, Airbus and other aerospace manufacturers to build aircraft largely without tariffs.

"Everybody is saying, 'We don't know who pays, but under no circumstances is it going to be us,'" said Richard Aboulafia, managing director of AeroDynamic Advisory, an aerospace consulting firm. "That's a dangerous thing, because you could have the things freeze up."

Write to Sharon Terlep at sharon.terlep@wsj.com

data.title: Urals diffs unchanged in quiet trade, May loading dates awaited
data.description:
Urals crude oil differentials remained stable on Tuesday amid low trading activity, while traders waited for loading dates for the grade in May.

Urals oil loadings next month are expected to remain rather thin as refinery maintenance plan doesn't show a rise in idle capacity for May leaving less volumes for exports, though the plan might be revised later this month, traders said.

Global oil demand will grow at its slowest rate for five years in 2025 and U.S. production rises will also taper off due to U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs on trading partners and their retaliatory moves, the International Energy Agency said.

PLATTS WINDOW

  • No bids or offers were shown for Urals, CPC Blend and Azeri BTC in the Platts window on Tuesday.

NEWS

  • The petrochemical plant at Austrian oil and gas company OMV's Schwechat refinery near Vienna suffered an outage on Tuesday, causing it to switch to high-flare operations that alarmed passersby and prompted calls to the fire brigade.

  • Pakistan is considering importing crude oil from the United States for the first time to offset a trade imbalance that triggered higher U.S. tariffs, according to a government source directly involved with the proposal and a refinery executive.

data.title: Caterpillar Names COO Joseph Creed as Next CEO
data.description:
Caterpillar on Tuesday named Joseph Creed as the company's next chief executive, succeeding D. James Umpleby III, effective May 1.

Creed, who is a 28-year Caterpillar veteran, currently serves as chief operating officer. Umpleby, who also serves as the company's chairman, will become executive chairman following the leadership change.

"In 2024, Caterpillar achieved record full-year adjusted profit per share, which increased more than sixfold during Umpleby's tenure as CEO," the construction equipment supplier said in a statement.

Shares of the company were down 1.7% in late Tuesday afternoon trade. The stock has slid about 19% over the past 12 months.

At the end of January, the company said it was expecting slightly lower full-year revenue compared with 2024, according to a FactSet transcript of an earnings conference call. The consensus on FactSet is for full-year sales of $63.55 billion, down from last year's $64.81 billion, which reflected a 3% annual drop.

The company on Jan. 30 projected lower year-on-year top line for the first quarter. Analysts are looking for revenue of $14.71 billion for the March quarter, down from $15.8 billion a year earlier.

data.title: Top Hegseth adviser Dan Caldwell put on leave in Pentagon leak probe
data.description:
One of U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's leading advisers, Dan Caldwell, was escorted from the Pentagon on Tuesday after being identified during an investigation into leaks at the Department of Defense, a U.S. official told Reuters.

Caldwell was placed on administrative leave for "an unauthorized disclosure," the official said, speaking on condition of anonymity. The decision has not been previously reported.

"The investigation remains ongoing," the official said without providing details about the nature of the alleged disclosure, including whether it was made to a journalist or to someone else.

President Donald Trump's administration has moved to aggressively pursue leaks, an effort that has been enthusiastically embraced by Hegseth at the Pentagon.

A March 21 memo signed by Hegseth's chief of staff, Joe Kasper, requested an investigation into "recent unauthorized disclosures of national security information involving sensitive communications."

Kasper's memo left open the possibility of a polygraph, although it was unclear if Caldwell was subjected to one.

Although Caldwell is not as well known as other senior Pentagon officials, he has played a critical role as an adviser to Hegseth.

His importance was underscored in a leaked text chain on Signal disclosed by The Atlantic last month.

In it, Hegseth named Caldwell as the best staff point of contact for the National Security Council as it prepared for the launch of strikes against the Houthis in Yemen.

Caldwell had drawn attention in Washington for past views that critics have called isolationist, but which advocates said sought to right-size America's defense priorities.

A Marine Corps veteran who deployed to Iraq, Caldwell was quoted saying before going to the Pentagon that America would have been better off if U.S. troops had just stayed home.

"I think the Iraq war was a monstrous crime," Caldwell told the Financial Times in December 2024.

He was also a skeptic of U.S. military assistance to Ukraine and advocated for U.S. retrenchment from Europe.

The decision to put Caldwell on administrative leave is separate from a wave of firings since Hegseth, a former Fox News host and combat veteran, took over the Pentagon in January.

Those firings of top brass have included the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, the top admiral in the Navy, the head of U.S. Cyber Command and the top U.S. military lawyers. Reuters was first to report last week the firing of the U.S. military representative to the NATO Military Committee.

data.title: Learn Option: The Greeks
data.description:
Learn the Option Greeks: Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega & Rho Explained

Welcome to Article 4 of the Learn Options Series, a key step in your journey toward mastering stock options education. Today, we introduce you to the Option Greeks, the core metrics that help you measure and manage risk, probability, and time sensitivity in options trading.

If you're learning options seriously, understanding the Greeks is essential. These metrics allow you to evaluate not just whether an option is likely to be profitable, but also how it will react to changes in the market.

What Are Option Greeks?

The Greeks. But not those Greeks. Options Greeks.

"Option Greeks" refer to mathematical values that describe the sensitivity of an option’s price to different factors. They are essential tools in options trading 101 for understanding how and why option prices move.

Let’s break them down:

1. Delta (Δ) – Sensitivity to Price Movement

  • What It Tells You: How much the option price moves when the underlying stock price moves by $1.

  • Call Option Delta: Between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.60 means the call option gains $0.60 when the stock goes up $1)

  • Put Option Delta: Between 0 and -1 (e.g., -0.40 means the put option gains $0.40 when the stock drops $1)

Real-World Example: A call option on stock XYZ has a delta of 0.65. If XYZ rises from $100 to $101, the option price increases approximately $0.65, assuming no other changes.

Why It Matters:

  • Helps estimate profit potential

  • Useful in building delta-neutral strategies (advanced)

  • Crucial for learning directional exposure in your options portfolio

2. Gamma (Γ) – Sensitivity of Delta to Price Changes

  • What It Tells You: How much delta changes when the stock price moves by $1

  • High Gamma: Means delta changes quickly; more risk and more reward

  • Low Gamma: Delta changes slowly; stable exposure

Example: If Gamma is 0.10 and Delta is 0.40, and the stock moves up $1, the new Delta becomes 0.50.

Why It Matters:

  • Important for short-term options traders

  • Tells you how stable your delta exposure is

  • Useful when managing fast-moving trades

3. Theta (Θ) – Sensitivity to Time Decay

  • What It Tells You: How much the option loses in value each day, all else equal

  • Always Negative for Long Options: Because time decay hurts buyers

Example: A call option has a Theta of -0.08. Each day that passes, the option loses $0.08 in value—assuming nothing else changes.

Why It Matters:

  • Time decay accelerates near expiration

  • Vital for income strategies (e.g., covered calls, credit spreads)

  • Crucial for managing expectations on long option trades

4. Vega (V) – Sensitivity to Volatility

  • What It Tells You: How much the option’s price will change if implied volatility changes by 1%

Example: If Vega is 0.12, and implied volatility rises by 1%, the option price increases by $0.12

Why It Matters:

  • Helps you understand risk from volatility

  • Crucial in earnings trades or high-volatility markets

  • Selling options in high IV environments (and buying in low IV) is a classic strategy

5. Rho (ρ) – Sensitivity to Interest Rates

  • What It Tells You: How much the option’s price changes when interest rates shift by 1%

Example: If Rho is 0.03 for a call, and interest rates rise by 1%, the option’s price will increase by $0.03.

Why It Matters:

  • Often minor, but can matter for longer-dated options (LEAPS)

  • Becomes more relevant in high-rate or rate-changing environments

Option Greeks Summary Table

GreekMeasures Sensitivity ToAffectsApplies ToImpact Type
DeltaStock priceDirectional profit/lossCalls & Puts$/Share move
GammaDelta changeStability & reactivityCalls & PutsChange in Delta
ThetaTime decayPremium erosionCalls & Puts$ lost per day
VegaImplied volatilityPrice swingsCalls & Puts$ per 1% IV move
RhoInterest ratesLong-term pricingCalls > Puts$ per 1% rate change

How to Use Greeks in Real-World Learning Options

  • Theta Management: Be aware of time decay when holding long options close to expiration.

  • Delta Adjustment: Choose Deltas that match your confidence level in directional trades.

  • Vega Awareness: Avoid buying high-IV options unless you expect IV to rise further.

  • Gamma Trading: Short-term trades = higher gamma = higher risk/reward.

  • Portfolio Management: Combine Greeks to manage overall portfolio exposure.

Wrapping Up Your Introduction to Option Greeks in Stock Options Education

Understanding Greeks is like reading the engine behind your trades. These tools help you anticipate changes in your option's value, understand risks better, and construct more balanced strategies.

In our next article in the Learn Options Series, we’ll build on this knowledge with vertical spreads—a structured way to trade direction while managing risk.

Stay on track with your options trading 101 journey at ForexLive.com (evolving to investingLive.com later this year), where stock options education is clear, structured, and designed to help you succeed.

data.title: Ball in China's court for trade talks, White House says
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is open to making a trade deal with China but Beijing should make the first move, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Tuesday.

"The ball is in China's court: China needs to make a deal with us, we don't have to make a deal with them," Leavitt told a press briefing, saying Trump had given her that statement directly in an Oval Office meeting to use.

"China wants what we have ... the American consumer, or to put another way, they need our money," Leavitt said.

China raised its tariffs on imports of U.S. goods to 125% on Friday in a retaliatory move to Trump, who effectively raised U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods to 145%, while putting a pause on planned levies for other countries' goods.

Trump has described Chinese President Xi Jinping in admiring terms, but neither man has backed down in an escalating trade war between their two countries.

"The president, again, has made it quite clear that he's open to a deal with China. But China needs to make a deal with the United States of America," Leavitt said.

Trump has said he expects something positive to come out of the trade tensions between the world's two largest economies. But, unlike multiple other nations who have responded to his plans for tariffs by seeking deals with Washington, Beijing has raised its own levies on U.S. goods and not sought talks.

data.title: ROCKET LAB USA (RKLB) CLASS ACTION DEADLINE APPROACHING: Berger Montague Advises Investors to Inquire About a Securities Fraud Class Action by April 28, 2025
data.description:
Philadelphia, Pennsylvania--(Newsfile Corp. - April 15, 2025) - Berger Montague PC advises investors that a securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Rocket Lab USA, Inc. ("Rocket Lab" or the "Company") on behalf of purchasers of Rocket Lab securities between November 12, 2024 through February 25, 2025, inclusive (the "Class Period").

Investor Deadline: Investors who purchased or acquired ROCKET LAB securities during the Class Period may, no later than APRIL 28, 2025, seek to be appointed as a lead plaintiff representative of the class. To learn your rights, CLICK HERE.

On February 25, 2025, Bleecker Street Research published a report accusing Rocket Lab of misleading investors about the likelihood that its Neutron rocket will launch in mid-2025. The report revealed that the Company's plans for three barge landing tests, which were originally scheduled to occur in a window between September 2024 and March 2025, had been pushed back to at least September 2025 and could occur as late as March 2026.

The report further revealed significant delays in preparing the Company's launch pad, including a potable water problem not scheduled to be fixed until January 2026.

The report also alleged that the Company's only Neutron contract so far is a discount deal with an "unreliable startup" named E-Space.

On this news, Rocket Lab's stock price fell $2.21, nearly 10%, to close at $20.28 per share on February 25, 2025.

To learn your rights or for more information, CLICK HERE or please contact Berger Montague: Andrew Abramowitz at aabramowitz@bm.net or (215) 875-3015, or Peter Hamner at phamner@bm.net.

A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of all class members in directing the litigation. The lead plaintiff is usually the investor or small group of investors who have the largest financial interest and who are also adequate and typical of the proposed class of investors. The lead plaintiff selects counsel to represent the lead plaintiff and the class and these attorneys, if approved by the court, are lead or class counsel. Your ability to share in any recovery is not, however, affected by the decision whether or not to serve as a lead plaintiff. Communicating with any counsel is not necessary to participate or share in any recovery achieved in this case. Any member of the purported class may move the Court to serve as a lead plaintiff through counsel of his/her choice, or may choose to do nothing and remain an inactive class member.

Berger Montague, with offices in Philadelphia, Minneapolis, Delaware, Washington, D.C., San Diego, San Francisco and Chicago, has been a pioneer in securities class action litigation since its founding in 1970. Berger Montague has represented individual and institutional investors for over five decades and serves as lead counsel in courts throughout the United States.

data.title: Soybeans slip from highs on weak export demand, trade tensions
data.description:
Key points:
  • Soy pulls back from recent high on weak export demand
  • Corn rally peters out on unimpressive demand
  • Wheat falls on predictions of rain relief in the U.S. Plains

Chicago soybean futures fell on Tuesday, a day after hitting a seven-week high, under pressure from weak demand from top buyer China amid an escalating trade war and Brazil's bumper soybean harvest.

Corn futures ticked lower on lackluster demand, though an export sale of U.S. corn to Portugal on Tuesday morning helped support prices. Wheat futures edged lower for a second day after weather forecasts predicted better growing conditions in the U.S. wheat belt, while tensions with major trading partners continued to weigh heavily on the market.

"Demand is just not there. I think buyers are just waiting for a new crop to come along from other nations and choosing not to buy from the U.S.," Tom Fritz, broker at EFG Group, said. Fritz noted that the White House's sweeping tariffs on major trading partners have given foreign buyers little incentive to purchase U.S. products.

All three crops were supported by a weak dollar, which makes U.S. farm goods cheaper for overseas buyers. The U.S. dollar index is sitting near three-year lows after U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff policies shook global markets.

The most-active soybeans on the Chicago Board of Trade settled down 5-3/4 cents at $10.36 per bushel.

Wheat settled down 5-1/2 cents to $5.42 per bushel while corn settled down 3-3/4 cents to $4.81-1/4 per bushel.

Weather forecasts predicted much-needed rain next week in the U.S. Plains, as well as in cropping regions in France and Germany.

"The rain on the way is exactly what the doctor ordered," Fritz said.

The USDA in a weekly crop progress report on Monday rated 47% of the U.S. winter wheat crop in good to excellent condition, down from 48% a week earlier, while the agency reported spring planting of corn and soybeans was in the beginning stages.

  • Excess rains have delayed corn seeding in parts of the U.S.

flooidCX Corp. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Quantum Energy Corporation, formerly known as flooidCX Corp., has released its annual 10-K report, detailing significant financial improvements, strategic initiatives, and operational highlights. The company, which has pivoted from customer-business communications to the development and sale of private electricity generating equipment, is positioning itself as a leader in sustainable energy solutions.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $6.5 million, primarily generated from the sale of distributor licenses.
  • Operating Income (Loss): $6,015,571, showing a significant improvement from the previous period due to increased revenue from license sales.
  • Net Income from Continuing Operations: $793,677, despite a substantial loss on debt settlement, the company achieved positive net income.
  • Net Income (Loss): $793,677, marking a profitable year in 2023 compared to a net loss in the previous period.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Common Shares - Basic: $0.03, indicating positive earnings per share for the year ended December 31, 2023.

Business Highlights

  • Corporate History and Name Changes: The company, originally incorporated as Baixo Relocation Services, Inc. in 2014, underwent several name changes, becoming Gripevine Inc. in 2016, flooidCX Corp. in 2019, and most recently Quantum Energy Corporation in 2024, reflecting its strategic shifts and rebranding efforts.
  • Change in Control and Strategic Pivot: In July 2022, flooidCX Corp. experienced a change in control when MP Special Purpose Corp. acquired a significant ownership stake. This led to a strategic pivot from customer-business communications to the development and sale of private electricity generating equipment, emphasizing distributed energy owned by consumers.
  • Business Operations and Technology: Quantum Energy Corporation is at the forefront of developing 100% distributed direct electrical energy systems. The company holds exclusive licenses for technologies such as Harvested and Thermal Electrical Energy Collection and Transmission (EET), Photon Lighting Systems, and Photon Engines, which are setting new standards in energy efficiency and sustainability.
  • Market Opportunity: The company is positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for sustainable and efficient energy solutions driven by global efforts to reduce carbon emissions. Its unique product offerings, developed by research engineer Dennis M. Danzik, include technologies that allow various applications to harvest light and generate energy.
  • Product Innovations: Quantum's proprietary EET technology enables the transmission of harvested electrical energy through USB or ethernet cables, significantly reducing construction costs by eliminating the need for traditional electrical infrastructure. The SAFEwatt ethernet power adapter is an example of a product that reduces power consumption for devices like laptops.
  • Photon Lighting and Photovoltaic Systems: The company's Photon Lighting System transforms lighting fixtures into energy generators, while its photovoltaic systems are lighter and offer self-cleaning options compared to conventional solar systems.
  • Thermal Energy Harvesting and Photon Engines: Quantum is advancing thermal energy harvesting technologies and Photon Engines, which convert minimal input power into clean electrical energy, showcasing innovation in energy generation.
  • Rare Earth and Magnet Production: The company is involved in recycling and processing rare earth materials, essential for high-quality magnets used in various industries, further supporting its commitment to energy innovation.
  • Marketing Strategies: Quantum's marketing strategy focuses on positioning itself as a leader in the sustainable energy market, leveraging its technological innovations and commitment to sustainability. The company employs digital marketing, trade shows, and strategic partnerships to expand its market reach.
  • Future Outlook: The company projects significant growth in installation fees and recurring revenue through 2025, with plans to increase potential installation fees to over $150 million and recurring revenue contracts to exceed $100 million. This growth is contingent on securing customer and investor capital for installations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Market Expansion: The company has focused on expanding its market presence through the sale of exclusive and non-exclusive Licensed Distributorships, generating significant revenue.
  • Strategic Partnerships: Quantum has engaged in strategic partnerships, such as the cross-licensing agreement with Inductance Energy Corporation.
  • Merger Activities: The company has been involved in merger activities, although a recent merger was terminated.
  • Capital Management: The company has managed its capital through the sale of distributor licenses, which has been its primary revenue stream. It has also settled outstanding debt obligations by issuing common shares, resulting in a loss on debt settlement. The company has not declared or paid any dividends and does not anticipate doing so in the near future. The company has also restructured its equity, including the cancellation of Series A preferred shares and the issuance of Series B and C preferred shares as part of strategic transactions.
  • Future Outlook: The company projects an increase in potential installation fees and recurring revenue contracts in the coming years, with plans to fund these through customer or investor capital. It anticipates expanding its Direct Energy System installations and has several pending contracts and letters of intent that could significantly boost its revenue. The company is also exploring new markets and opportunities for growth, particularly in the Philippines and other regions.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: The transition from customer-business communications to the development and sale of private electricity generating equipment presents operational risks as the company adapts to a new market and product line.
  • Financial Challenges: The company faces several financial challenges, including a significant increase in operating expenses, primarily due to increased business operations and terminated merger activity. The company reported a loss on debt settlement of approximately $5,094,000, which significantly impacted other expenses. Additionally, the company's liquidity is limited, and its ability to meet capital demands is contingent on securing customer or investor financing for installations. The company has limited plans to offer equity or take on additional debt, which could constrain its ability to finance future growth.
  • Market Risks: The company's exposure to market risks, such as fluctuations in energy demand and regulatory changes, could impact its financial performance. The company's reliance on customer and investor capital for installations also presents a financial risk if such funding is not secured.

data.title: PATRIOT NATIONAL BANCORP INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Patriot National Bancorp Inc., a one-bank holding company for Patriot Bank, N.A., headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest and Dividend Income: $52.4 million, a decrease from $59.0 million in 2023, primarily due to a reduction in average loan balances and narrower net interest margin.

Net Interest Income: $20.1 million, down from $28.5 million in 2023, with a decline in net interest margin to 2.1% from 2.8% due to higher deposit costs and increased nonaccrual loans.

Provision for Credit Losses: $12.5 million, up from $7.4 million in 2023, reflecting selective management of credit exposure and significant charge-offs.

Total Non-interest Income: $8.4 million, an increase from $6.0 million in 2023, driven by higher income from the digital payments program.

Total Non-interest Expense: $32.1 million, a slight decrease from $32.7 million in 2023, primarily due to the absence of a goodwill impairment charge recorded in 2023.

Net Loss: $39.9 million, compared to a net loss of $4.2 million in 2023, significantly impacted by a $25.1 million full valuation allowance on deferred tax assets.

Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: $(10.03), compared to $(1.05) in 2023, reflecting the increased net loss.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Patriot National Bancorp, Inc. operates as a one-bank holding company for Patriot Bank, N.A., which is headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut. The bank has eight branch offices across Connecticut and New York, focusing on commercial real estate, business loans, and SBA loans.

Geographical Performance: The bank's lending activities are primarily concentrated in Fairfield and New Haven Counties in Connecticut and Westchester County in New York. The commercial real estate loan portfolio is heavily concentrated in New York, accounting for 49.61% of the total, followed by Connecticut at 23.44%.

Product Lines: The bank offers a range of financial products, including commercial real estate loans, SBA loans, and consumer loans. In 2024, the bank reentered the residential mortgage business, originating loans through its Residential Mortgage Division in Jacksonville, Florida.

Digital Payments Division: The Digital Payments Division is a significant source of lower-cost deposit balances and non-interest income, working with program managers to process high-volume payment transactions.

Operational Changes: In 2024, the bank focused on reducing its loan portfolio to strengthen capital ratios, resulting in a decrease in gross loans held for investment by $141.4 million.

New Product Launches: The bank reentered the residential mortgage business in 2024, originating mortgage loans through its Residential Mortgage Division.

Future Outlook: The bank is working towards meeting regulatory capital requirements set by the OCC, with a focus on strategic planning, capital planning, and risk management. The Private Placement completed in March 2025 is expected to improve the bank's capital ratios.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company focused on reducing its average and risk-based assets to improve capital ratios, resulting in a decrease of $124.4 million in average assets. The company also entered into a Program Management Agreement for its Digital Payments Division, which involves originating credit card loans marketed by a buyer, enhancing non-interest income and liquidity.

Capital Management: The company significantly reduced its borrowings from $201.1 million in 2023 to $33.1 million in 2024, repaying $70 million of BTFP borrowings and reducing FHLB advances. Shareholders' equity decreased by $40.1 million due to a net loss of $39.9 million. The company did not pay dividends over the recent three-year period but may consider resuming in the future. The company also obtained an interest deferral on its senior notes until 2025.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its asset reduction strategy to meet regulatory capital requirements set by the OCC, which include maintaining specific capital ratios. The Private Placement is expected to improve capital ratios, and the company aims to generate profits to potentially reduce the valuation allowance on deferred tax assets. The company will also focus on strategic planning, capital planning, and risk management as part of its agreement with the OCC.

Challenges and Risks

Economic and Market Conditions: The company is vulnerable to national and local economic downturns, particularly in Connecticut and New York, which could lead to increased loan delinquencies and reduced demand for banking products.

Interest Rate Risk: Fluctuations in interest rates can negatively impact the bank's financial performance, affecting net interest spreads and the value of its securities portfolio.

Credit Risk: The commercial real estate loan portfolio poses a material risk due to its reliance on the successful operation of properties and borrowers' income streams. Declines in real estate values could further exacerbate this risk.

Operational Risks: The loss of key management personnel could impair customer relationships and business operations. Additionally, cybersecurity threats pose a significant risk to operations and reputation.

Regulatory Risks: Changes in banking regulations and compliance requirements could adversely affect profitability and growth. The company is also subject to environmental liability risks associated with its lending activities.

Liquidity Risk: The ability to generate sufficient cash flows to support operations and fulfill obligations is critical, especially given the reliance on deposits and borrowings.

Asset Quality: The allowance for credit losses decreased significantly due to charge-offs from large commercial real estate loans, indicating potential weaknesses in asset quality.

Capital and Liquidity: The company has focused on increasing liquidity and strengthening capital ratios by reducing loan growth and increasing deposits.

Nonperforming Assets: An increase in non-accrual loans and nonperforming assets suggests heightened credit risk, necessitating careful management of loan portfolios.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, particularly interest rate fluctuations, which can impact the profitability of its loan and investment portfolios. Management has implemented strategies to mitigate these risks, but their effectiveness is not guaranteed.

data.title: CFN Enterprises Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
CFN Enterprises Inc., a company operating in the cannabis, hemp, and wellness industries, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing significant financial improvements, strategic initiatives, and ongoing challenges. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's performance, including the impact of its recent acquisition and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenues: CFN Enterprises reported net revenues of $20.2 million, a substantial increase from $3.5 million in 2023. This growth was primarily driven by the full-year operations of its Ranco subsidiary.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $5.3 million, up from $0.5 million in 2023, reflecting improved cost management and increased revenue.

Loss from Operations: The loss from operations was $(2.5) million, a significant improvement from $(13.8) million in 2023, due to increased revenue and reduced impairment expenses.

Net Loss: CFN Enterprises reported a net loss of $(4.3) million, an improvement from $(15.2) million in 2023, driven by higher gross profit and lower operating expenses.

Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities: The company generated $0.4 million in net cash from operating activities, compared to a net cash usage of $(5.0) million in 2023, indicating better cash management and operational efficiency.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: CFN Enterprises operates two primary businesses: CFN Media, a digital marketing agency for the cannabis, hemp, and wellness industries, and Ranco, a white label manufacturing and co-packing business. The company is also launching an e-commerce network for general wellness CBD products.

Ranco Acquisition: On July 1, 2023, Ranco, LLC acquired assets from RAN CoPacking Solutions LLC, enhancing its capabilities in co-packing, white label manufacturing, and third-party logistics services.

Licensing Agreement: Ranco entered into a Licensing Agreement with PW Industries LLC and others for exclusive manufacturing, packaging, and distribution of hemp-based and nicotine-based products, effective for five years.

CFN Media Operations: CFN Media generates revenue through compliant ad campaigns and sponsored content, targeting the cannabis, hemp, and wellness industries. It operates several media platforms to reach investors and educate them about the cannabis industry.

Ranco Operations: Ranco provides white label manufacturing and co-packing services, including ordering and shipping custom packaging and hardware from overseas, and offering third-party logistics services.

Regulatory Environment: The CFN Business is regulated by the SEC, FINRA, and cannabis regulations, while Ranco operates under the Farm Bill, which distinguishes hemp from marijuana and allows for its legal production and marketing.

Employee Overview: As of December 31, 2024, the company employed 22 full-time employees, excluding independent contractors, with no collective bargaining agreements in place.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on the CFN and Ranco businesses, pursue strategic transactions, and expand its e-commerce network for CBD products, despite facing challenges related to its financial condition and regulatory environment.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Focus: CFN Enterprises has strategically focused on expanding its operations through the acquisition of assets from RAN CoPacking Solutions LLC, enhancing its capabilities in co-packing and white label manufacturing services. This acquisition includes comprehensive solutions for third-party logistics, custom packaging, and strategic marketing support.

Capital Management: The company has engaged in several capital management activities, including issuing promissory notes to raise $5 million, which were later rolled over to Ranco, LLC. These notes are secured by Ranco's assets and are subject to mandatory repayments. The company also issued shares of common stock as part of the Ranco business combination and to settle outstanding accrued interest on Series B Preferred Stock. Furthermore, the company has a working capital deficit and is managing its liquidity by raising capital through debt and equity, while also focusing on reducing operating and overhead costs.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue as a going concern by raising additional capital, growing its Ranco Business, and managing costs. It is also exploring strategic transactions and opportunities, including the expansion of its e-commerce network for CBD products. However, the company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to its working capital deficit and accumulated losses, necessitating ongoing efforts to secure financial stability.

Challenges and Risks

Regulatory Challenges: The company operates in the highly regulated cannabis, hemp, and wellness industries, which are subject to complex and evolving regulations. The CFN Business is regulated by the SEC, FINRA, and federal and state cannabis regulations, while the Ranco Business is affected by the Farm Bill and state-level regulations on hemp products. The company must navigate these regulatory environments to maintain compliance and avoid potential legal issues.

Financial Challenges: CFN Enterprises faces significant financial challenges, including a history of losses, substantial indebtedness, and a working capital deficit. As of December 31, 2024, the company had a working capital deficit of $19.2 million and an accumulated deficit of $78.9 million. The company's ability to continue as a going concern is in doubt, as expressed by its independent registered public accounting firm.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in demand for its products and services, competition from other marketing service providers, and adverse macroeconomic and geopolitical conditions. The cannabis industry remains illegal under federal law, posing legal risks to the company's operations and its clients.

Operational Risks: Operational risks include the company's reliance on third-party computer hardware and software, potential security breaches, and the need to attract and retain key personnel. The company also faces challenges in increasing brand awareness and attracting new customers.

Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are significant, with the company subject to numerous laws and regulations related to the cannabis and CBD industries. Changes in these regulations could impact the company's operations and financial results. The company also faces potential litigation risks associated with the CBD industry.

Management Strategies: The company is focused on growing its existing business acquired under the Ranco Agreement and launching an e-commerce network for CBD products. Management plans to raise capital, manage costs, and pursue strategic transactions to address financial challenges. However, substantial doubt remains about the company's ability to continue as a going concern.

Market Risk Disclosures: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and potential changes in trade policies and tariffs. These factors could impact the company's costs, demand for its products, and overall financial condition.

data.title: ALPHA MODUS HOLDINGS, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Alpha Modus Holdings, Inc., a technology service provider specializing in solutions for retailers and consumer brands, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive AI technology market. This comprehensive summary provides insights into Alpha Modus' transition from a blank check company to a technology innovator, its financial health, and its future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 million. Alpha Modus had no revenue during the years ended December 31, 2024, and 2023.
  • Operating Expenses: $834,895, an increase from $391,949 in 2023, primarily due to an increase in professional fees related to the pending merger and increased accounting and legal fees.
  • Net Income: $4.1 million, compared to a net loss of $0.5 million in 2023, primarily due to the gain in change in fair value of earnout shares of $18.7 million, offset by a forbearance fee expense of $13.2 million.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.73 basic, $0.13 diluted, reflecting the impact of the gain in change in fair value of earnout shares and other financial adjustments.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Alpha Modus Holdings, Inc. transitioned from a blank check company to a technology service provider following a business combination with Alpha Modus, Corp. The company focuses on technology solutions for retailers and consumer brands, leveraging IBM's Bluemix platform and recognized by IBM Watson for innovation.
  • Core Technology and Services: Alpha Modus offers end-to-end patented solutions aimed at enhancing consumer experiences at the point of sale. The technology includes targeted marketing campaigns, inventory control, smart planograms, and consumer behavior analysis.
  • Patent Portfolio: The company has developed a robust patent portfolio, including the '571 patent family, which covers various methods for monitoring and analyzing consumer behavior in retail environments. This portfolio is central to Alpha Modus' strategy to license its technology to third parties.
  • Acquisition of eyeQ: In 2018, Alpha Modus acquired eyeQ, an Austin-based startup, to enhance its technology offerings. This acquisition included eyeQ's assets and pending patent applications, which have since evolved into a comprehensive patent family.
  • Licensing Agreements: In 2024, Alpha Modus entered into licensing agreements with GZ6G Technologies Corp. and Xalles Holdings Inc., allowing these companies to use Alpha Modus' patented technology for promotional and operational functions.
  • Patent Infringement Actions: The company initiated several patent infringement lawsuits against major retailers, including The Kroger Company and Walgreen Co., to enforce its patent rights and protect its intellectual property.
  • Future Outlook: Alpha Modus plans to expand its patent enforcement efforts and develop an internal sales and marketing team to drive licensing revenue. The company aims to leverage its patent portfolio to secure partnerships and enhance its market position.
  • Operational Strategy: With a focus on licensing, Alpha Modus intends to maintain a lean operations team, concentrating resources on research and development to support its patent portfolio and drive innovation in retail technology.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Partnerships and Licensing Agreements: Alpha Modus has focused on strategic partnerships and licensing agreements to expand its technological offerings. Notably, it entered into a license agreement with GZ6G Technologies Corp. to co-develop AI-driven advertising solutions and with Xalles Holdings Inc. for exclusive use of its patented intellectual property in self-service kiosks. The company is also actively pursuing patent infringement actions to protect its intellectual property, which is a key strategic initiative to safeguard and monetize its innovations.
  • Capital Management: The company completed a business combination with Insight Acquisition Corp., resulting in a reverse recapitalization. This transaction included the issuance of 5,295,000 shares of common stock and 7,500,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock. Additionally, Alpha Modus entered into a securities purchase agreement with Streeterville Capital, LLC, issuing a secured convertible promissory note for $2,890,000 to enhance liquidity. The company also managed its capital structure by issuing shares for services and note extensions, and it has a financing arrangement for an insurance policy.
  • Future Outlook: Alpha Modus plans to continue its intellectual property licensing and enforcement efforts throughout 2025. The company aims to deploy services under its existing license agreements by the end of 2024 and expand its service offerings in additional industries. It also intends to seek additional funding through increased revenues and future financings to support its growth strategy. The company is focused on maintaining its plan of growth, which includes raising a minimum of an additional $2,500,000 to cover expected expenses and support its strategic initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operating Losses and Cash Flow: Alpha Modus faces significant risks that could impact its business operations and financial condition. The company has experienced operating losses and negative cash flows, with no assurance of achieving profitability in the future.
  • Competitive Market: The competitive and rapidly evolving AI technology market poses a risk, as larger competitors with more resources could outpace Alpha Modus in innovation and market presence.
  • Going Concern: The company’s ability to continue as a going concern is in doubt, as highlighted by the going concern qualification in its financial statements, indicating potential insolvency without additional financing.
  • Intellectual Property Risks: Alpha Modus is exposed to significant intellectual property risks, including challenges in obtaining, maintaining, and protecting its intellectual property rights. The evolving patent laws and potential for costly litigation further exacerbate these risks.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company incurs increased costs due to its status as a public company. Compliance with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act and other regulations requires substantial financial and management resources, which could divert attention from core business activities. The company’s management team has limited experience managing a Nasdaq-listed public company, which could impact its ability to effectively navigate these regulatory challenges.
  • Market Risks: Alpha Modus is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in the trading price of its common stock, which could be affected by various factors such as investor perceptions, market conditions, and the company’s financial performance. The potential for delisting from Nasdaq due to non-compliance with listing requirements poses an additional risk, which could impact the liquidity and marketability of its securities.

SEC Filing: ALPHA MODUS HOLDINGS, INC. [ INAQ ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

data.title: Basis bids steady, farmers wait for rain
data.description:
Spot basis bids for hard red winter (HRW) wheat were flat on Tuesday in the southern U.S. Plains, dealers said.

* Farmers are hoping for rain to ease dryness in the region, and showers are expected over the weekend. It's unclear how beneficial they will be yet after crop conditions recently deteriorated, a broker said.

* In Kansas, the top producer of hard red winter wheat, 43% of the crop was rated good to excellent as of Sunday, down from 51% a week ago, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.

* K.C. May hard red winter wheat futures (KWK25) ended down 2-1/4 cents at $5.53 a bushel after earlier setting its lowest level since March 31.

data.title: GERN INVESTOR DEADLINE: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP Announces that Geron Corporation Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Investor Class Action Lawsuit
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP announces that purchasers or acquirers of Geron Corporation securities between February 28, 2024 and February 25, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), have until Monday, May 12, 2025 to seek appointment as lead plaintiff of the Geron class action lawsuit. Captioned Dabestani v. Geron Corporation, No. 25-cv-02507 (N.D. Cal.), the Geron class action lawsuit charges Geron and certain of Geron’s top current and former executives with violations of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. A subsequently filed complaint is captioned Potvin v. Geron Corporation, No. 25-cv-02563 (N.D. Cal.).

If you suffered substantial losses and wish to serve as lead plaintiff of the Geron class action lawsuit, please provide your information here:

https://www.rgrdlaw.com/cases-geron-corporation-class-action-lawsuit-gern.html

You can also contact attorneys J.C. Sanchez or Jennifer N. Caringal of Robbins Geller by calling 800/449-4900 or via e-mail at info@rgrdlaw.com.

CASE ALLEGATIONS: Geron is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company that focuses on the development of therapeutic products for oncology. According to the complaint, Geron’s primary product is a telomerase inhibitor, imetelstat, which Geron sells under the brand name Rytelo.

The Geron class action lawsuit alleges that defendants throughout the Class Period made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (i) defendants created the false impression that they possessed reliable information pertaining to Geron’s projected revenue outlook and anticipated growth while also minimizing risk from seasonality and macroeconomic fluctuations; (ii) in truth, Geron’s optimistic reports of Rytelo’s launch success and potential growth fell short of reality as the impacts of seasonality, existing competition, and the burden of continued monitoring played a much more significant role in patient starts than defendants had implied; and (iii) Rytelo lacked the necessary awareness to penetrate the market, resulting in an inability for Geron to capitalize on the purportedly significant unmet need for the drug, particularly among first-line patients and those outside the academic setting.

The Geron class action lawsuit further alleges that on February 26, 2025, Geron announced its financial results for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2024, disclosing that Rytelo’s growth had flattened over the preceding months, attributing the diminished growth on seasonality, competition, lack of awareness for Rytelo, and the burden of the monitoring requirement necessary for the drug treatment. On this news, Geron’s stock price declined more than 32%, the complaint alleges.

THE LEAD PLAINTIFF PROCESS: The Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 permits any investor who purchased or acquired Geron securities during the Class Period to seek appointment as lead plaintiff in the Geron class action lawsuit. A lead plaintiff is generally the movant with the greatest financial interest in the relief sought by the putative class who is also typical and adequate of the putative class. A lead plaintiff acts on behalf of all other class members in directing the Geron class action lawsuit. The lead plaintiff can select a law firm of its choice to litigate the Geron class action lawsuit. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff of the Geron class action lawsuit.

ABOUT ROBBINS GELLER: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP is one of the world’s leading law firms representing investors in securities fraud and shareholder litigation. Our Firm has been ranked #1 in the ISS Securities Class Action Services rankings for four out of the last five years for securing the most monetary relief for investors. In 2024, we recovered over $2.5 billion for investors in securities-related class action cases – more than the next five law firms combined, according to ISS. With 200 lawyers in 10 offices, Robbins Geller is one of the largest plaintiffs’ firms in the world, and the Firm’s attorneys have obtained many of the largest securities class action recoveries in history, including the largest ever – $7.2 billion – in In re Enron Corp. Sec. Litig. Please visit the following page for more information:

Past results do not guarantee future outcomes. 

Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices.
